narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 p.A2730V (alanine to valine at codon 2730) is a missense change in the BRCA2 gene that has been reported in the context of somatic alterations in cancer. In a 2019 study of primary cutaneous melanomas, this variant was identified once among the cohort, representing 0.9% of the somatic mutations detected in that series [PMC6863855].

No clinical case reports, segregation analyses, or formal clinical classifications for the p.A2730V variant were presented in the extracted evidence, and therefore no clinical data are available to inform its role in hereditary cancer predisposition.

The available evidence does not include any functional assays or experimental studies evaluating the impact of the p.A2730V substitution on BRCA2 protein function, DNA repair activity, or cellular phenotypes.

Population data are limited to the single observation in the melanoma cohort, indicating that the variant is rare in that specific tumor sample set. No broader population allele frequency or in silico predictive scores are provided in the extracted material.

Given the solitary report of p.A2730V as a somatic event in melanoma and the absence of clinical, functional, or computational data, the current evidence is insufficient to assign a definitive pathogenic or benign classification. At present, the variant would be considered of uncertain significance pending further investigation.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study surveys somatic mutations in primary cutaneous melanomas and reports a single occurrence of the BRCA2 p.A2730V variant among many others identified in the cohort.
article_mentions.title: A comprehensive evaluation of pathogenic mutations in primary cutaneous melanomas, including the identification of novel loss-of-function variants
